name: Fnb Picayune Bank
description_short: FNB Picayune Bank is a community bank serving Pearl River, Hancock, and Stone counties in Mississippi since 1947, offering personal and business banking services.
description_long:
FNB Picayune Bank was established in 1947 and operates as a hometown community bank focused on serving residents and businesses in Pearl River County, Hancock County, Stone County, and surrounding areas in Mississippi. The bank emphasizes personal relationships and local decision-making as core to its business model.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of personal banking services including checking and savings accounts, online banking with enrollment and password management, debit/ATM card services (DEBIT MASTERCARD®), mobile banking, text banking, bill pay, electronic statements, and loan payment processing. For business customers, they provide business checking, business savings, commercial services, and lending options. The institution also maintains a dedicated lending department with personal, business, and general lending services available through their loan team.

FNB Picayune Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on community banking and customer service rather than size or technological innovation. The bank has maintained continuous operation for over 75 years in the same geographic region, suggesting stability and local market knowledge. They offer modern digital conveniences including mobile banking, text banking, and Apple Pay integration alongside traditional services.

As a small regional bank, FNB Picayune Bank is best suited for customers who prioritize local banking relationships and community focus over extensive branch networks or cutting-edge fintech features. The bank's service area appears geographically limited to a specific region of Mississippi. No information about account minimums, interest rates, fees, or specific loan terms is disclosed on their website, which may require direct contact for detailed comparison shopping.
services: Online banking with enrollment and login management
DEBIT MASTERCARD® (combination checkbook and ATM card)
pros: Established local bank with 75+ years of continuous operation since 1947
Offers modern digital banking services including mobile banking, text banking, and Apple Pay
Provides both personal and business banking services with dedicated lending department
Community-focused approach with emphasis on customer service and local relationships
Accepts DEBIT MASTERCARD® usable in over 100,000 locations worldwide
Convenient multi-channel access via phone, telebanking, online banking, and in-person
Bill pay and electronic statements for account management convenience
cons: Geographically limited service area restricted to three Mississippi counties and surrounding areas
Website lacks detailed information about account minimums, fees, interest rates, or APR terms
No disclosure of FDIC insurance status, assets under management, or institution size
Limited information about loan products, terms, rates, or qualification requirements
No mention of physical branch locations, hours of operation, or ATM network details
best_for: Residents of Pearl River, Hancock, or Stone counties seeking personal banking relationships
Small businesses in the service area needing local lending decisions and community connections
Customers who prioritize in-person service and local bank stability over digital-first banking
Individuals seeking DEBIT MASTERCARD® with nationwide and international acceptance
diagnosis: FNB Picayune Bank is best for Mississippi residents in its three-county service area who value community relationships and personal banking service over extensive digital features or widespread branch access. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this bank only serves a specific region of Mississippi, making it unsuitable for customers outside Pearl River, Hancock, and Stone counties.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
